item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! That's some really high praise and it means a lot, I'm thrilled you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. I love large-scale SCPs like this when they're well executed, and this knocks it out of the park.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahaha, yeah, I really hate those two and wanted to give them some comeuppance

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your feedback! I'm glad you enjoyed it overall, and I'm thinking about some ideas for the last addendum.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you so so much! This such high praise and it made my day to hear it. Writing this I was worried the length would turn people off, and it's really good to know it didn't lose you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Site director Gladys Morrison"

I see what you did there… Anyway, love a good marine horror story and there aren't anywhere near as many Aussie SCPs as there should be, so take my +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally thought this was a really good piece, I really enjoyed the more Lovecraftian aspects of the SCP. However I have to agree the with a couple other people the last addendum was not needed in the article. Nonetheless great work good job keeping this long article entertaining and well paced. +1

Body horror, psychological horror, deep sea horror, giant monsters, MTFs getting mulched. Really classic style SCP, like the sort I used to read which got me into the site in the first place. This is brilliantly paced and phrased too: even though it's a longer article I never got bored and wanted to keep reading. In a lot of scips audio logs and camera transcriptions are used as an excuse to just switch to unrealistic narrative writing, but I absolutely buy the dialogue and descriptions in your logs. The images really help too.

Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback! the answer is pretty much as said below, that they are being seen and the foundation has a lot of work to do in covering it up. This was largely based on the really high number of ufo sightings over bass strait. My thinking is that they generally stay quite high up so the lights are the most visible part of them.
As for the military action aspect, bass strait is very lightly patrolled by the navy (We don't have a coastguard) since it's a really dangerous stretch of water irl and a lot of ships sink there yearly. Besides fishing boats and the Spirit of Tasmania, there's just not many people out on the water there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they are being seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just don't get this one. It's the scout units that kill it for me.

They're big, weird-looking, slow-moving blimps in the sky during broad daylight. If everyone can see them from a distance, how come nobody can escape from them, or shoot them down, or capture pictures or video of them?

Does Australia not have a coast guard? Or an air force? Or tourists with smartphones?

I could maybe buy it if they were sneaking up on people underwater, then springing out at point blank range? But these things are visible for kilometers around! You're telling me Crocodile Dundee over here can deal with jellyfish, sharks, snakes, spiders, stingrays, parliament, and scorpions on a daily basis, but they can't get the drop on a balloon that has to go back underwater in order to breathe? What happens if they go after a guy on the shore, while there's another guy a kilometer inland with a ute?

Everyone can see it! It's not even small!

I know you say it attacks people when they're alone, but you'd have to be literally the only person on the entire beach for them to get away with it.

How are they not seen? How are they not constantly seen by everybody in northern Tasmania? Foundation webcrawlers alone must be constantly scrubbing social media to keep this shit covered up. It's just so visible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I'm thrilled that you enjoyed it!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Update: I made that last comment before I finished reading the whole thing, and wow that ended at a MUCH bigger scale than I was expecting. My +1 remains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how SCP-4159 makes a cameo, it reminds me of those "Monster Island" scenes from the old Godzilla movies where all the kaiju are together in one place haha

+1
Thank you guys so much. I never thought one of my articles would be so well liked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's totally fair! I did feel it was important to the story so hopefully in the future I can find a way to make it pop a little better.
Still, I appreciate your taking the time to give some feedback, thank you very much and I'm really happy you enjoyed it overall!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one… except! That last addendum (all the tabs, not any one alone) seems superfluous and flattens it out somehow.

Following the harrowing experience of that dive with a battlescene just jarred a bit.

Personal response. Still enjoyable though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's fair enough! Thank you for the feedback and reading all the way through :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ultimately this felt a lot like 835, but the tone, length and pacing weren't as clean and tight as the latter. I can see you put a ton of effort into this, though, and I was compelled to read through it in its entirety. I feel like, if it could be tightened up (perhaps losing the radio log from the pilot, or radically condensing it and conjoining it with the black box recovery log, condensing the dive log, etc) I'd upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes sense. Nice thing you built here, probably my favorite SCP at the moment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I was implying a few things there. Firstly that this thing is so massive that it's spread over a very large area, and that it can move beneath the sand to reach out into the open water wherever it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just to clarify, SCP-5007-C tentacles can go through the ground and emerge from the seafloor outside SCP-5007-B? I got a little confused there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It comes from the land down under

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same! Aquatic skips have always been my favorite. The ocean is so mysterious and creepy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disturbing things? Yes

Are those things trying to kill you? Yes

Australia

+1

Better ask to the COG guys for a Nuke Strike…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea why I love anomalous sealife so much… but this does a great job +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn, I have no idea how or why I made that bit repeat itself, thanks for pointing it out, it's fixed now.
thank oyu very much for the kind words! Being compared to Junji Ito is high praise indeed! As for Price saying "Let's go home", I'm not going to say it wasn't intentional…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t know if it was intentional, but the way that Addendum 5000-02 ends with

Sgt. Price: Let's just go home.

is absolutely hilarious. It’s not often that a spooky SCP literally makes me laugh out loud, so congrats.

Beyond that, this is definitely a spooky SCP! The monsters are like something straight out of Junji Ito’s The Hanging Balloons, and -C reminds me of the leech monster from 1730. Which is to say, this is an SCP that will keep me up at night.

In terms of writing criticisms, I will echo the earlier point about “on the dd/mm/yy” being clunky, and point out that the intro to Site 40-R Recovered Footage repeats itself almost verbatim.

On the whole, I’m a big fan, and enthusiastically give you my +1!

item.title: Re: NUKING IS NOT ENOUGH
item.content:encoded: considering that the scp-5000 instances are literally made of human remains and that significant amounts of human dna were found in the strange organic substance, i think it's entirely possible and very likely that 096 would regard it as at least acceptably human. and the email proves that it might have sentience, so if human dna isn't what sets him off then surely it's "anything sentient capable of seeing."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good catch, thank you! I fixed it now.
I'm super glad you enjoyed this, and I'm happy I was able to make you jump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very very very nitpicky nitpick: in the manned exploration log, in the bit of d-class dialogue with the [04:19:26] timestamp, theres a colon missing on the D-class's designation.
Very enthusiastic +1 overall, I have a very specific phobia for eye related horror and deep ocean horror and this scratched that itch with a sharpened rake. The "I SEE YOU" email actually made me jump.

item.title: Re: NUKING IS NOT ENOUGH
item.content:encoded:
Why destroy it though? It's more or less effectively contained, and this is the SCP Foundation, not the Global Occult Coalition.
As for the use of 096, are you sure it would work? We don't know if animals seen by mr shy makes him wook out, and this thing is very very far from human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It ended in an 05 note which in hindsight didn't need to be there. The last tab as a whole was far less detailed and didn't do as good a job of explaining what happened to the site, but also in conveying the severity of the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What was the old ending?

item.title: NUKING IS NOT ENOUGH
item.content:encoded: The Foundation has access to railguns and stuff, correct? I say this calls for Neutralization by antimatter device consisting of at least 50o grams of antimatter, delivered by railgun. And yes, i know ONE gram of antimatter is said to contain enough energy to power the east cost of the USA for like a week, but this thing needs to be rendered GONE ASAP. Or it has eyes, right? test scp 096 on the thing. Just Kill it, kill it with absolute prejudice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahaha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe those are just called Australians actually

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I'm actually working on a new ending which should be out within 24 hours that will hopefully address this.
I'm very glad you enjoyed the overall story though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad about that, I didn't actually know about 3477 when I wrote this haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very good, old-fashioned eldritch horror. I have to agree, though, that some of it could be trimmed, especially the O5 note at the end, which is unnecessary and frankly cheesy. If you could find a way to end with the "FOUND YOU" message, that would be a much more effective way to end it. Still, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[05:34:21]: One set of partial remains floats in front of the SCPS Nautilus, looking directly at the camera. Later analysis confirmed this to be the remains of former Australian Prime Minister Harold Holt. It mouths something to the camera, unable to be deciphered by Foundation lip readers.

The funny thing is, this can absolutely work in continuity with SCP-3477.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of your current four tabs, I see it going like this:

- Tab 1 is your current tab 2.
- Tab 2 is your current tab 3.
- Tab 3 then becomes "prior to the attack on Site-40-R, all accounts recieved an e-mail" etc., and then at the bottom, you have a collapsible that's your standard "Authorized eyes only" (ooh, maybe with similar phrasing!) and it opens up into that "FOUND YOU" eyeball because it's hella creepy.

Whether you keep the message from O5-4 is up to you, but I'm not sure where it would be best used or even if it's necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely thrilled to hear you enjoyed it. I'm really glad you liked that I made the UFOs a comparatively better option here haha. Shaping 5000-A as a big eye wasn't my original intention, but that's sure as hell cannon now!
I would like to better understand what you mean about viewing classified data at the end. What would the classified data be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is some classic creepy ocean shit! I love how you connected these various disappeared Australians by explaining the UFO sightings are something considerably worse than UFOs.

I do agree that the final note from O5-4 isn't really helpful, to the article as a whole or as an ending. I like the idea of detailing the assault on Site-40-R, and then ending with some kind of "view classified data" thing to wrap up on a scary note.

Am I right in thinking that 5000-A, with its giant black pit in the center, is shaped like an eye? :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed it! I do agree it could be trimmed and do intend on doing so at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I feel that you could have cut this down a bit (I'd argue that several of the middle logs of abductions are only reinforcing already established abilities and behavior) the mystery as to what these creatures are, how they got here, and what they are doing is quite engaging.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a possibility…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this may be way off base, as this is just a little inkling I have, but is this related in any way with fifthism? There is a lot of themes of "melding" and a lot of "4"s show up throughout.

It's probably just me talking crazy, but I was just struck by the possibility of this being some leftover creature from like a proto-fifthist "fourth church" or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I'm glad you picked up on the easter eggs, you're the first one to do so! Will look into fixing up or removing the note at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was pretty damn engaging all the way through, full of good bits of dialogue, little easter eggs, and creepy elder horrors. Good stuff Balth, +1.

If I had to offer one piece of crit, it would be that the O5 note was wholly unnecessary and could probably be cut wholesale without losing much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thank you so much! What's funny is that I didn't start this intending to make it eldritch horror it just kind of happened lol.
I'm super glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's been a while since anything on the wiki so succintly GOT eldritch horror, and of course, here in the 5000 contest, here's an entry that… just does EVERYTHING right. and with a jumpscare even!

+1, best of luck in the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely feel you. I actually agonised over that decision for about a week. Wrote both versions and showed it around to a bunch of people to see what they preferred and ultimately came out with this.
Thank you so much for taking the time to read this and to comment your feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t find the last note particularly helpful. I think the rest of it was pretty good, but I’m wondering if I prefer the whole thing be left at FOUND YOU (the note). It would’ve been a lot more anticipatory than the clean conclusion, albeit the conclusion itself is effective in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you much! For the praise and for the correction!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small spelling error, during the manned D class submarine log, a line reads "God I can wait to get back up", I'm assuming that's supposed to be can't wait.

That was really good, things just kept getting worse and worse and the article got better and better. Love the monster design, reminds me of the Engineers from the Halo games combined with the monster from Neil Blomkamp's short film "Zygote". Terrifying!

Big +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see you

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the lovely people at reddit made this meme of this SCP. I am beyond thrilled that someone liked this enough to make this, thank you!

Meme Time.
Close

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gosh, that's some strong praise! Thank you so much! I'm absolutely thrilled that you enjoyed it, and I'm really glad for the feedback. No matter how many times you run it through word some errors still slip in haha.
